payload.text: Independent review C3 (Critical, confirmed live): cmd_premise --supersedes refuses only live/unverified old premises, so a RETRACTED premise can be redirected to a fresh claim -- with NO human gate on the verb. An agent (no TRUTH_HUMAN) redirected a human-retracted P0 premise and RELEASED the blocked work while the claim stayed terminal. Paper section 1 says retraction cannot be undone by any later event; ADR-013 lists retracted among supersede-eligible dead states -> cross-surface contradiction on the headline property, defended at the status layer, spent at the readiness layer. Fix (ADR-017): superseding a retracted premise requires the ADR-011 human gate (TRUTH_HUMAN + typed-id ack), symmetric with the authority that imposed the retraction; mechanical dead states (stale/diverged/cannot_verify/missing) stay ungated. Amends ADR-013 intake. Canary + core test; paper section-1 terminality clarified.

payload.title: M1: cross-surface version-stamp drift (schema $id v0.7 vs shape, README title, paper status line, README ADR file-tree) — reconcile + mechanize the version cross-check
M2: schema permits records the prose forbids — claim.anchor_commit minLength 1 vs 7 (tighten, lockstep); document the schema's necessary-not-sufficient role re ADR-009 screened

M2c (adversary-surfaced): recheck_verdict defaults a missing stored returncode to the current run's rc, so a hand-appended rc-sensitive empty-output record can never rc-diverge at recheck — verification-strength residual, not a safety gap (ADR-027 discloses it)

evidence.command: grep -q "truth-ledger-record.v0.9" template/.truth/schema/claims.schema.json && grep -q test_schema_shape_fingerprint template/scripts/test-truth-core.py && grep -q test_readme_title_equals_cli_version template/scripts/test-truth-core.py && grep -q "claims ledger (v0.9.8)" template/.truth/README.md && echo OK

payload.text: ADR-026 ships the schema-contract version policy and mechanizes the two hard version stamps: the schema $id is set to truth-ledger-record.v0.9 (bumped on record-shape change, independent of the product version), a core test pins the README title to the CLI version, and a shape-fingerprint core test breaks when the schema changes so a shape change cannot skip the $id bump; the README title, README file-tree ADR listing, and paper status line are reconciled reference-not-restate

evidence.command: grep -q "FAULT AN5" template/scripts/truth-canary.sh && grep -q "claim anchor_commit must be a git" template/scripts/truth && grep -q "VERIFIED claim must carry a real anchor" template/.truth/schema/claims.schema.json && echo OK

payload.text: ADR-027 tightens the anchor_commit and commit floor to a git SHA prefix of at least 7 chars in schema and validate mirror lockstep across claim, verdict, and invalidation, forbids a null anchor on a VERIFIED claim, and documents the schema as a necessary-not-sufficient gate deliberately silent on the ADR-009 evidence-screen semantics that are enforced operationally at filing and recheck; corpus fixtures plus canary FAULT AN1 through AN5 gate the floor

payload.basis: M1 shipped as ADR-026 (tr-22853f21): schema $id=v0.9 + README==CLI and shape-fingerprint core tests + reference-not-restate stamps; battery green
M2 shipped as ADR-027 (tr-b60196bf): anchor floor lockstep across three kinds + VERIFIED-null forbidden + necessary-not-sufficient disclosure; canary AN1-AN5, battery green
